title: Level Instant Gratification (Joke)
wikitext:
{{Joke}} [[Category:Joke Levels]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Anomalous Levels]] {{Transparent Theme}} {{Background|file=download (15).jpeg}}
{{Class_4|safety=Not Very Safe|security=Super Duper Unsecure|entity=Mentally Affecting TikTok Kids Present}} '''Level Instant Gratification''' is an anomalous level in the Backrooms. {{Audio|file=instantplus.mp3|volume=1}} ==
I - Description
== [[File:Download (16).jpeg|thumb|An image of one of the TikTokers in this level.]] [[File:Jimmytheloser.jpeg|thumb|An image of the brainrot the TikTokers believe.]] [[File:Download (29).jpeg|thumb|An image of the level during nighttime.]] This level seems to be a level similar to that of the underwater world of [https://spongebob.fandom.com/wiki/Encyclopedia_SpongeBobia '''SpongeBob SquarePants'''] for some odd reason; nobody really knows why. If you are not familiar with the underwater world of SpongeBob SquarePants, then here are things broken down for you: The level consists of sand on the ground, flowers in the blue sky for some odd reason, and a small town with houses and roads. This level takes place underwater, but for some reason you can breathe in the water, for there is a special type of drug that activates inside you when you enter this level known as pseudopulmium. Pseudopulmium is a special drug that, when activated, causes you to be able to breathe underwater for an indefinite time (it ends when you exit the level). There have been no documented side effects to this drug so far; however, reporters have recently had their suspicion raised at the fact that around 38% of the people who exited this level have reported having either a headache or dizziness. This level clones itself in a way when a wanderer finds their way into it, these being called instances. Each instance looks the exact same; the only difference being that each instance has a different wanderer in it. This makes it impossible to have multiple wanderers in the same instance so that the only help one can get with completing this level are the hints and references they may encounter beforehand. However, when one person leaves or dies, another can join that same instance later; it doesn't just delete itself on exit or death of a wanderer. Upon entering this level, you may be met with a few people-looking fish. These fish have been documented to be chronically addicted to TikTok. Interacting with these fish is useless, for nearly all of them cannot hold a conversation with anyone for more than five straight seconds without dozing back off into the crap they're addicted to. If you look at one of the fish's phone they are holding, you will notice that the content they watch is pure brainrot. They seem to believe and accept everything they see on there, so the majority of them will not live past the age of thirty. One anonymous wanderer reported seeing one of these fish believing a TikTok video that said "Ever tired of a cold fork on a winter day? Just put the fork in a microwave and watch it heat up to a nice temperature for eating with!" The houses the fish live in are tall and cylindrical with lots of windows. These houses have been documented to be made out of scrap metal and come in a wide variety of colors ranging from gray to white to even dark red. There are a few select houses that don't look like this, however. The three most known houses in this level that don't look like this look like a yellow pineapple, a dark-blue moai, and a maroon rock. There is music that plays in the background of this level; however, the music isn't the typical relaxing SpongeBob SquarePants music you'd expect from this underwater world. Why, the only song anyone has ever reported hearing is the very song that is playing in the background right now--that is, if you haven't muted it already. As probably anticipated, there is no exact documented location this music comes from. The music actually starts off very faint when you first arrive into the level. There is no way to get this music to stop; the residents here get driven insane by this (maybe not; considering how addicted they are to their screens, they probably haven't even noticed the music yet). After only twenty minutes of one being in this level, nighttime will occur, causing the internet to shut off and every brainrotten individual to go insane now that their devices don't work anymore. These individuals are so stupid that they blame you for the loss of internet and start attacking you. The music in the background will ramp up to an annoyingly loud volume once this happens. This is when you should seriously consider finding your way out of this level, for nighttime is permanent once it occurs. Hiding in a resident's house will not work, however, for breaking in causes the resident to get so angry that they will come charging toward you trying to knock you dead. However, there is one place in the whole level that is safe to stay in; that being a restaurant known as the Krusty Krab. The Krusty Krab is a restaurant that looks like a crab trap (I know, pretty ironic) and sells these burgers known as Krabby Patties. In this level, eating these Krabby Patties will poison you and cause you to pass out. Most wanderers die within an hour of eating these patties due to the high amount of toxins present in the burger. Anyway, it is not recommended to stay in the Krusty Krab for long, for the brainrotten individuals will start piling up at the entrance making it impossible to escape. You should stay in for less than five minutes only for catching your breath after running around for too long if you don't want to die by the individuals. You can also come back later if you run out of breath again. Running out into the abyss away from the town will get you pushed back by a special force. This force is very strong--almost as strong as gravity--and will push you out and onto the ground commonly causing injuries. It has been documented by a few reporters that this force gets stronger the harder you push against it making it impossible to get past it. When it turns nighttime, there is about a 5% chance that an individual has a psychological disorder known as Internet Dependency Disorder, or in simpler terms, IDD. When the internet shuts off at night, these individuals will be able to run at double the speed and deal far mare damage than what a normal individual would do. It is guaranteed that an IDD individual will be there on creation of an instance. If none of the individuals make the 5% chance on creation, the level will automatically make a random individual have the disorder no matter what. You will notice almost instantly where these individuals with IDD are when it turns nighttime because of how fast they run. You should always seek immediate shelter at the Krusty Krab if you have nowhere else to go and are completely out of breath; you should never stand out in the open trying to catch your breath, for the IDD individuals will always find their way to you to try and kill you. A few select houses around the map have a large, green dumpster behind each of them. There is one dumpster that this level possesses which contains an ax you can use to defend yourself against the individuals. All you have to do is swing the ax forcefully at one of the individuals to kill them; however, this will only work on around ten individuals, for the ax will break if you try to kill more. Once the ax is gone, it will never come back. It is recommended to use the ax only on either IDD individuals or just anybody who is on the verge of killing you in general. Exits in this level are quite difficult to find for the average nonobservant person. If you don't want explicit answers on where to find an exit, try to find an anomaly present in this level. If you notice something off with a specific portion of the level, whether it would be a door floating in mid-air for example, you should try to interact with it if you want to possibly escape this level. ==
II - Entities
== The only entity that is currently present and that has always been present in this level is the fish, also known as the brainrotten individual or simply just the individual. There have not been any other documented entities that have ever been present in this level, although a few reporters have been debating recently between whether pseudopulmium counts as an entity, an object, or a phenomenon. ==
III - Bases, Colonies, and Outposts
== No matter what, any bases, colonies, or outposts cannot be established at all in this level due to the nature of no permanent safe spot existing here and the fact that not more than one person can be in an instance at once. ==
IV - Entrances and Exits
== ===
IV.I - Entrances
=== *Entering a door labeled "INT. GRAT." on '''[[Level 19]]''' will lead a wanderer to this level. ===
IV.II - Exits
=== The exits present in this level only actually allow you to exit through them once nighttime occurs. Each individual who goes through an exit will be met up with whoever was with them before entering the level--that is, if the level they enter allows for multiple wanderers to be in the same instance at once. *Entering a wooden door at the very edge of this level will lead a wanderer to '''[[Level 334]]'''. *Driving a purple car into the abyss will lead a wanderer to '''[[Level 20]]'''. This is the only way to get past the border. Make sure you are driving at top speed if you want to actually succeed in doing this, though. The reason this is the only car that makes it past the border is because this is the only car that can reach a top speed of over a hundred kilometers per hour which is the required speed to reach the other side. ==
V - Addendum
==
Recovered Note I honestly do not know how long I've been stuck in this level at all. The only thing I know is that nighttime occurred all of a sudden around twenty minutes ago, and now everyone's aggravated at me. No matter what, anywhere I hide, these screen-addicted human fish won't stop hunting me down to wherever I find myself. Certain fish will charge at me if I enter a building, which is why I stopped attempting that, although there is one crab-trap-shaped restaurant I can safely hide in for just a brief amount of time before the fish start piling up at the entrance, locking me in. Not to mention that the food here is absolutely horrible; whenever the burgers this restaurant has touches my taste buds, I feel like I could pass out onto the floor never to wake up again. There are a few anomalies I have noticed in this level, those being an oddly purple car and a random wooden door. Alright, so here's everything that just happened: I went into the car, drove down to the border present in this level that is supposed to push you back into the level to prevent escaping, and rammed through it. Then, I somehow ended up in this warehouse. I am actually quite sure that this warehouse I'm in is a different level.
Most reporters assume by the note that the person who wrote it was referring to this level, considering they mention the crab-trap-shaped restaurant, the screen-addicted human fish, the border, the purple car, and exiting into '''[[Level 20]]''' when they used the purple car to drive past the border.
Author: [[User:Omaclintock|Omaclintock]]
